_comment: Fixture: network endpoints exercised by `cargo fetch` / `cargo build`
against an empty cache on Rust 1.70+ (sparse-index default).
Source of truth (documentation):
- cargo 1.70 release notes (sparse protocol default),
- https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/registry-index.html#web-api,
- https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/environment-variables.html
for the default `CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse` setting.
Source of truth (live verification — 2026-04-25):
Booted a sandbox VM with a hand-rolled policy permitting Ubuntu apt
mirrors + the three documented cargo hosts. apt-installed cargo
1.75.0 inside the VM, then ran `cargo new` + `cargo add serde@1` +
`cargo fetch -v` against an empty CARGO_HOME. Captured mitmproxy
events on the gateway data plane and filtered to the cargo-phase
window via an RFC3339 timestamp marker. Observed cargo-phase hosts:
- index.crates.io  (sparse index lookups)
- static.crates.io (crate tarball downloads, ~7 crates pulled)
Did NOT observe `crates.io` (apex) — sparse mode bypasses the web-API
redirector and goes straight to the CDN. The apex is retained in the
preset because it remains required for `cargo publish`, `cargo
search`, `cargo yank`, and any tooling that hits the JSON web API.
Findings artifact (one-off): /tmp/cargo-fetch-trace.json.
Note: an earlier verification policy also pre-allowed the Fastly
Anycast supernets (151.101.0.0/16, 146.75.0.0/17) as a workaround
for a DNS-rotation race. Synchronous DNS-policy gating closed that
race architecturally, so the verification policy no longer needs
the CIDR pool — the host-only allow set is sufficient.
This file freezes the set of hosts AND the per-host method posture
the `cargo` built-in preset must allow. The drift-detection unit
test in `src/presets/builtin.rs` (see
`cargo_preset_matches_frozen_trace`) fails if either the preset's
host list OR its per-host method set diverges from this fixture,
forcing a conscious update with a corresponding spec/docs review
(D-9 + "Known gaps" bullet).
Per-host `methods` semantics — IMPORTANT:
The `methods` array reflects the methods the consume-only `cargo`
preset is intended to ALLOW for each host, not the broader set of
methods the host's API technically accepts. Cargo's preset is a
fetch-flow allow-list — POST / PUT / DELETE on `crates.io` exist
for `cargo publish` / `cargo yank` / owner management, but those
workflows are deliberately scoped to an explicit grant outside the
preset. Keeping `methods` here in sync with the preset's emitted
filters is what gives the drift test its signal: a future preset
change that adds POST to `crates.io` (or a fixture re-capture that
discovers cargo now sends PATCH to the sparse index) requires a
matched edit to both this file and `expand_cargo`, with a
rationale documented in the spec.
If a future cargo release introduces new telemetry / metrics hosts
(e.g. a sparse-index health-check endpoint or a publish-time
verification host), regenerate by running the live-trace methodology
above against the new toolchain and update both this fixture AND the
preset's host list in src/presets/builtin.rs::expand_cargo.

hosts.purpose: Sparse-protocol registry index. Default since Rust 1.70.
hosts.notes: Replaces the older git-based registry on crates.io-index.git. Cargo hits this first to resolve versions. Confirmed live (2026-04-25): observed during `cargo fetch` cargo-phase window.

hosts.purpose: Registry web API: search, yank, publish, owner management, and the `/api/v1/crates/<name>/<version>/download` redirector.
hosts.notes: Sparse-mode `cargo fetch` does NOT contact this host (verified live 2026-04-25 — sparse fetch goes index.crates.io → static.crates.io directly). Retained in preset because the `crates.io` apex still serves the JSON web API for `cargo search` and the legacy download redirector — both consume-only flows. The host's API also accepts POST / PUT / DELETE for `cargo publish` / `cargo yank` / owner management, but those mutating flows are deliberately OUT of the consume-only preset's scope; they require an explicit grant outside the built-in. `methods` here therefore encodes the preset's intent (consume-only), not the host's full capability surface.

hosts.purpose: CDN host that serves crate tarballs and (historically) RSS feeds.
hosts.notes: Confirmed live (2026-04-25): every crate tarball download (serde, serde_core, serde_derive, syn, quote, proc-macro2, unicode-ident) hit this host. Fronted by Fastly Anycast (151.101.0.0/16, 146.75.0.0/17). Synchronous DNS-policy gating closed the DNS-rotation race architecturally; the preset no longer pre-allows the Fastly supernets (the earlier workaround was retired).
